vocab.example: I'm constantly looking out for limited-edition sneakers that might drop on the official website.
I usually only buy new footwear when I finally have to do away with my old, battered trainers.
I rarely buy shoes online because I prefer to try them on in-store to ensure the fit is perfect.
Whenever my favorite brand brings out a new collection, I’m tempted to add another pair to my closet.
I tend to hold onto my high-quality leather boots for years, so I don't need to go shopping very often.
I don't go out specifically for shoes; I just buy them if I happen to come across a stunning pair while mall hopping.
I once stumbled upon a tiny boutique that sold handmade loafers, and I've been a regular customer ever since.
Buying cheap shoes is a false economy because they usually start to fall apart after just a few months.

vocab.meaning: tốn của ai đó bao nhiêu tiền
vocab.example: I only buy shoes occasionally because a decent pair of running shoes can set you back quite a bit of money.
I’m a fan of avant-garde fashion, so I buy shoes that really stand out and make a statement.
